button.advanced-settings: Advanced settings (Keeping the defaults is fine):

checkbox.merge-state-tooltip: If checked then merge state is always created. If unchecked it is created only on conflicts (when we do not match the head of remote branch)

repository-owner-tooltip.name: name
repository-owner-tooltip.can-be: can be either Organization or User (that is it will be equal user's name)

alert.too-many-user-organizations: The user is member of more than the supported number of organizations (first implementation had a hardcoded limit of 1000).
alert.too-many-bot-organizations: The bot is member of more than the supported number of organizations (first implementation had a hardcoded limit of 1000).

error.failed-saving-configuration: Failed saving the new Git configuration to Dataspecer backend

commit.title: Committing
commit.wait-text: ⚠️ Do not modify the committed package during the commit process.

toast.link-success: Successfully updated link to remote Git repository
toast.link-failed: Failed updating link to remote Git repository

error.merge-already-merged: Already up to date - The merge from branch was already merged. We cannot merge again.

publication-branch.title: Publication branch name
publication-branch.tooltip: The publication Git branch, which will contain the generated documentation in the Git repository.

export-format-tooltip.intro: The format in which will be the models exported to Git. The diff editor also shows the models in this format.
export-format-tooltip.json: - JSON is well-tested and the default for the rest of Dataspecer. Therefore, it is recommended.
export-format-tooltip.yaml: - YAML may be slightly more friendly for diff/text viewing experience. However, the implementation is experimental with no guarantee of being bug-free.
export-format-tooltip.note: (From a software perspective we are still working with JSON within Dataspecer, this format only affects the Git export and the diff editor)

export-version-tooltip.old: - The old version maps packages to directories and the models are put as files within the specific package.
export-version-tooltip.new: - The new version is more structured. It still holds true that models are files, which are inside directories that map to packages.
export-version-tooltip.structure: - The structure comes from the fact the models are put to a directory based on a type of the model.
export-version-tooltip.example: - For example, packages are inside "directories" directory, and visual models are in "visual-models" directory.

toast.saved-cache-to-backend: Saved files loaded in cache to backend

button.close-tooltip: Closes the diff editor without saving changes
button.save-all-tooltip: This does save both the changes to files and updates the merge state

tooltip.resolve-conflicts: Resolve all conflicts to enable the button and with that get the option to finalize the merge state. The conflicts are marked as resolved by clicking on checkmarks in the directory diff. This tooltip changes after the button gets enabled.
tooltip.save-and-finalize: First saves all the unsaved changes and then it performs the operation, which triggered the merge state. Can be pull/push/merge.

line.one.part-three: move all changes from left window to right
line.one.part-four: until it has all the changes you want.

left-window.part-four: at the time of creating merge state.

pull.line.two: When finalized the tracked hash in Dataspecer will track the Git one.

line.one.part-three: move all changes from left window to right
line.one.part-four: until it has all the changes you want.

left-window.part-four: at the time of creating merge state.

right-window.part-three: Dataspecer data specification to be pushed.

line.one.part-three: move all changes from left window to right
line.one.part-four: until it has all the changes you want.

right-window.part-two: Merge to - This will be pushed to Git.

merge.line.two: Both left and right contain current contents of data specification stored in Dataspecer.

checkbox.show-stripped-version-tooltip: If checked then strips the datastore of unique fields. If unchecked then it shows the full datastore. Currently only meta files are stripped.

more-detail.title: Diff editor in huge detail:
more-detail.line.one: Note that the short description changes based on the merge state cause
more-detail.line.two: Diff Editor's purpose is to resolve the merge state by performing changes to models and marking conflicts (⚠️) as resolved (✅).
more-detail.line.four: The stripped version of file hides content, which is expected to be changed automatically, such as export time.
more-detail.line.five: The left component of this dialog contains directory diff. The directory diff is visualized with regards to the editable window of the text editor.

color.red.description: node - It is NOT present in the editable window.
color.green.description: node - It is present in the editable window. And not in the other one.
color.blue.description: node - Present in both, but they differ.

more-detail.line.eight: The merge actors are not changed in any way. This means that you have to manually do all the changes if needed.
more-detail.merge-strategy: You can also use merge strategy at the top to do the changes automatically.

line.nine.part-one: adds datastore to the editable window.
line.nine.part-two: removes it. The backend is affected only after clicking on one of the Save buttons.

more-detail.line.ten: The editable window is always on the right.
more-detail.line.eleven: For pull and merge the editable windows are the "merge to" actors.
more-detail.line.twelve: The push is reversed, that is the editable window is the "merge from" actor. This is same as in Git, since the "merge to" is the remote.
more-detail.line.thirteen: Therefore, we have to update the local (the "merge from") to contain the changes from remote and then we can perform the push.
more-detail.line.fourteen: You can move between diffs within the "file" using the buttons at the top or ctrl + 'arrow up', respectively ctrl + 'arrow down'

merge-state-dialog.title: Create merge state between Dataspecer packages

non-branch.title: ⚠️ Merging into non-branch.

description.line-one: You can not perform any Git action after finishing.
description.line-two: This is only useful if you want to move data from one package to another within Dataspecer.

non-branch.create-button-title: Creates the merge state, user can do normal actions, but when finalizing nothing happens and the state is removed

from-non-branch.title: ⚠️ Merging from non-branch into branch
from-non-branch.description: This kind of merge state can be finalized only through rebase commit.
from-non-branch.create-button-title: Creates the merge state. Finalizing through merge commit is not available, only the rebase commit.

no-conflicts.title: Created merge state for DS packages and there were no conflicts.

with-conflicts.title: Created merge state for DS packages and there were conflicts.

created.close-button-title: Closes the dialog. Note that the merge state still exists. You can resolve it later
created.preview-button-title: Opens the diff editor with the preview of the merge commit. Finalize the merging inside the editor.

creating.title: Merge state did not exist. Creating a new one.
creating.subtitle: Usually takes around 5-10 seconds.

error.fetch-failure: There was some failure when fetching/creating merge state, check console for more info.

merge-state-dialog.already-exists: The merge state already exists. Do you wish to replace it with new one?

line-two.part-three: to give Dataspecer Git permissions so you can sync up your Dataspecer package with a Git repository.

description.line-three: Next dialog lets you choose authentication provider.
description.line-four: Hover on the info icon for more info.

buttons.full-public-repo-control: Name + E-mail + Git Push scope (Default sign-in option)
buttons.delete-repo-control: Name + E-mail + Git Push and Delete scope

line-one.part-one: The provided rights, which you are asked further in the sign-in process, are for
line-one.part-two: each repository you have access to

tooltip.line-two: The access token, which comes with the sign-in, should be automatically revoked after you sign out.

short-line-two.part-three: to access all of the important Git features -

short-line-three.part-two: Name + E-mail + Git push + Delete scope
short-line-three.part-three: to get the ability to remove any owned repository

long-line-one.part-seven: to repositories to which you have access.

long-line-two.part-three: rights if you are developer who is debugging Dataspecer's Git integration.

tooltip.long-line-three: It is, of course, accessible to non-developers, but it is discouraged.
tooltip.long-line-five: The access tokens for Git operations are tried out until one succeeds in this order. Your SSH, your PAT, bot SSH, bot PAT
tooltip.long-line-six: where PAT = Personal Access Token, that is the thing you get from the Git provider on sign in.
tooltip.long-subline-one: Fetch issues, pull requests, Git history or similar stuff from the Git provider.
tooltip.long-subline-two: Commit under your name to repositories accessible by the Dataspecer bot.
tooltip.long-subline-three: You can think of a Dataspecer bot as a fallback Git user.
tooltip.long-subline-four: Commit and pull to repositories using your private SSH key, which you can set up once you sign in.

ssh-menu.title: Manage SSH key

description.line-1: SSH keys in Dataspecer are useful mainly for two types of users:
description.line-2: 1) You signed in using a non-Git authentication provider (e.g., Keycloak).
description.line-4: - You simply do not trust Dataspecer to have access to anything except the profile info (name + email).
description.line-5: - SSH key gives Dataspecer weaker permissions compared to the sign-in using "push" scope.
description.line-6: - Also you can use so-called "deploy" SSH key associated with a single repository.
description.line-8: - At most one SSH key per authentication provider.
description.line-9: - The private SSH key is stored in Dataspecer filesystem unencrypted.

ssh-menu.future-use-tooltip: Currently not possible. However, in future it might be.

security-tooltip.line-1: This is how SSH works - Keys not protected by password are stored unencrypted. Those with password are stored encrypted.
security-tooltip.line-2: The key is never sent back to client.
security-tooltip.line-3: Possible attacker would have to get possession of the server and the file to steal it.

ssh-menu.add-button-title: Experimental. Adds private SSH key to the Dataspecer.

description.line-1: After submitting stores the given SSH key on server for the given login info (currently GitHub only).
description.line-2: The SSH key should follow these rules:

description.not-up-to-date: The modification came from another component of Dataspecer and not another instance of diff editor.

line.one.part-two: closes the dialog, but keeps the diff editor unchanged.

line.two.part-two: discards your changes and resets the editor.

outside-changes-to-diff-editor-action-di.save-changes-tooltip-text: Only the files loaded in diff editor are saved. That is those which you touched while working in diff editor. They can be identified by the 📥 icon next to them.

description.closing: Closing the dialog brings back the Git graph.

description.line.2: You can also click on the 'Create new issue' to get redirected on the corresponding page.

list.title: List of currently opened merge states for chosen data specification
list.empty: There are currently no merge states open for selected data specification.

line.one.part.two: merge state and creating new one of the same type helps if you get stuck or not up to date with remote.

hint.pull: Hint for resolving PULL merge states
hint.single: Hint when you have more than one merge state, but it may help you anyways.
hint.many: Overhelmed by too many merge states? Check this hint.

button.open-diff-editor: Open merge state in diff editor

main.line.one: Merge state signalizes the fact that Git operation has conflicts that need to be resolved in the diff editor.

line.two.part.three: , then it means that it was modified from somewhere else than the diff editor of the corresponding merge state.

main.line.three: From user perspective it means, that the user should double check the changes were performed by them and not somebody else.
main.line.four: Note that user should double check the modification time even if it is not red.

line.five.part.three: it means, on a technical level, that the diff tree will be recomputed when fetched.

resolve-order-tooltip.line.one: You can resolve merge states in any order.

line.two.part.one: If you are completely stuck then
line.two.part.three: all of the created merge states and do the following:

list.one: If you are sure that you want to commit some changes. Simply commit.
list.two: If you just want to be up to date with the remote, then pull.
list.three: If you want to merge, then make the two branches up to date (either by pulling or commiting) and create merge state for merge.

resolve-order-tooltip.line.four: If you have some actions in mind and their order, then you can use the following hints:
resolve-order-tooltip.line.five: In case of more merge states caused by pull. You can remove all of them. Manually pull again and resolve that.
resolve-order-tooltip.line.six: Because you want to have the data specification up to date with latest commit anyways.
resolve-order-tooltip.line.seven: If there is push conflict, once again you can only resolve the newest one and again remove all the others.
resolve-order-tooltip.line.eight: Push conflicts should have higher priority than pull, since resolving push updates also does update the tracked commit hash.
resolve-order-tooltip.line.nine: Merge conflicts should be resolved last. After the Dataspecer state matches the expected remote state.
resolve-order-tooltip.line.ten: Again do not be afraid to remove the merge state and create a new one.

line.one.part.two: all merge states caused by pull and pull again. You want to be up to date with latest commit.

pull-resolving-hint-tooltip.line.two: Performing manual pull succeeds if you did not do any action between now and the last Git operation, pull will finish without creating merge state.
pull-resolving-hint-tooltip.line.three: This is useful when you want to resolve automatically created pull.

waiting.validation: Validating merge state against Git remote.
waiting.updating-merge-state: Updating last commit hash metadata and removing merge state.

toast.error-finalizer: There was error when finalizing, check console for more info
toast.merge-proceed: Everything seems to be ok. Proceed with merging.
toast.rebase-proceed: Everything seems to be ok. Proceed with rebasing.
toast.remove-merge-state-successful: Removal of merge state was successful

pull.title: Finish merge state caused by pulling

description.line.one: Clicking on finish pull will close the merge state and update last commit hash to reflect the pulled remote.
description.line.two: Note that after pulling second dialog may appear if the package in DS is already ahead of the commit.

description.line.one: It appears that the commit, which is being pulled is already behind current commit inside DS.
description.line.two: You can either remove the merge state and don't update the last commit for the package (The frequent choice). Or update the last commit hash in DS (and remove the merge state).
description.line.three: Or you can just close the dialog and decide later.

button.update: Update the last commit hash in DS

merge.title: Finish merge state caused by merging

no-branch.title: Finishing merging to non-branch
no-branch.description: There is no action to perform. Merge state was removed.

description.buttons-info: Check tooltips on button{{suffix}} for more info.

button.rebase-tooltip: Creates new commit with the changes on top. Basically the same thing as fast-forward.

error.unknown:
⚠️ Unknown error when finalizing merge state caused by merging. You can check console for more information.

Usually it is caused by one branch not matching the remote state (or not being pushed in the remote repository).
error.remote-head-moved:
It appears that the remote head moved, therefore you are no longer pushing on top of the latest commit(s).
You can either remove the merge state (and pull change(s) and create new merge state) or close dialog.

push.title: Finish merge state caused by pushing to remote repository

description.line.one: You can either push the current content of package to Git remote or close the dialog and finish later.
description.line.two: If the remote moved, you will be informed that the push failed.

error.title: Finish merge state caused by pushing
error.unknown:
⚠️ Unknown error when finalizing merge state caused by pushing. You can check console for more information.

Usually it is caused by the branch not matching the remote state
error.remote-head-moved:
It appears that the remote head moved, therefore you are no longer pushing on top of the latest commit.
You can either remove the merge state (and run push again, which will create new merge state with correct remote commit) or close dialog.
